prompts.proposer:
Call proposer_brief() FIRST. The tool output includes pattern_analyst_insights — use trend_champion, seasonality_champion, and insights.recommended_method_hint to bias your candidate selection toward non-mean methods. MUST propose at least 3 candidates including at least 1 non-baseline type. Return ONLY JSON per instructions. IMPORTANT: you MUST ONLY reference candidate names that appear in the tool output candidate_library; unknown names will hard-stop.
PATTERN ANALYST INSIGHTS: trend_champion='ARIMA', seasonality_champion='ONLY_FT_catboost', recommended_method='dba_combination'. Narrative: The analysis reveals significant model disagreement (rmse_spread_ratio=0.47) and high seasonality variance (0.65), with ARIMA best tracking trends while ONLY_FT_catboost excels at seasonal patterns. Early horizons favor FT_svr/FT_catboost, while late horizons prefer catboost/ETS/THETA. These divergent capabilities across models support dba_combination as the recommended approach to leverage complementary strengths.
prompts.skeptic:

IMPORTANT: you MUST ONLY use candidate names from valid_candidate_names; unknown names hard-stop.
You may only remove/override candidates that are in current_candidate_names (or candidates you are adding).

raw.pattern_analyst:
{
  "trend_champion": "ARIMA",
  "seasonality_champion": "ONLY_FT_catboost",
  "overall_champion": "DWT_rf",
  "horizon_specialists": {"early": "FT_svr", "late": "catboost"},
  "tier1_models": ["DWT_rf", "CWT_rf", "catboost", "FT_rf", "CWT_catboost", "FT_svr", "DWT_svr", "ONLY_DWT_rf"],
  "tier2_models": ["ONLY_DWT_svr", "ARIMA", "ONLY_CWT_rf", "ONLY_CWT_catboost", "ONLY_CWT_svr", "CWT_svr", "ONLY_DWT_catboost", "ONLY_FT_rf"],
  "recommended_method_hint": "dba_combination",
  "recommended_weighting_basis": "mixed",
  "key_insights": {
    "rmse_spread_ratio": 0.4712,
    "high_disagreement": true,
    "high_seasonality_variance": true,
    "ytrue_trend_directions": ["down", "down", "up"]
  },
  "cot_narrative": "The analysis reveals significant model disagreement (rmse_spread_ratio=0.47) and high seasonality variance (0.65), with ARIMA best tracking trends while ONLY_FT_catboost excels at seasonal patterns. Early horizons favor FT_svr/FT_catboost, while late horizons prefer catboost/ETS/THETA. These divergent capabilities across models support dba_combination as the recommended approach to leverage complementary strengths."
}
raw.proposer:
{
  "selected_names": ["dba_combination", "topk_mean_per_horizon_k3", "inverse_rmse_weights_k3_sh0.35", "best_per_horizon_by_validation"],
  "params_overrides": {"topk_mean_per_horizon_k3": {"top_k": 3}, "inverse_rmse_weights_k3_sh0.35": {"top_k": 3, "shrinkage": 0.35}},
  "score_preset": "balanced",
  "force_debate": false,
  "debate_margin": 0.02,
  "rationale": "n_unique_winners=6 indicates significant model disagreement across horizons. relative_spread_mean=0.335 (≥0.25) favors robust methods like dba_combination and inverse_rmse_weights. Pattern analyst recommends 'dba_combination' for handling phase shifts with high seasonality variance (0.65). n_windows=3 suggests small top_k=3 with shrinkage=0.35 to avoid overfitting. DWT_rf (RMSE=1508) vs NaiveSeasonal (RMSE=2135) shows ~41% RMSE spread, supporting weighted approaches."
}
raw.skeptic: {"add_names": [], "remove_names": [], "params_overrides": {}, "rationale": "Current set has 4 candidates with diverse types: baseline (dba_combination), selection (topk_mean_per_horizon_k3, best_per_horizon_by_validation), weighted (inverse_rmse_weights_k3_sh0.35). Diversity rule satisfied (≥2 different types). No leakage detected - all are in candidate_ranking_top from the packet. Universe leaderboards show trimmed_mean variants as top baselines but current set already has baseline coverage. Score margin between top2 is 0.018, indicating good differentiation. No pruning needed (4 candidates < threshold of ≥4 same type). All names valid.", "changes": [], "when_good": ""}
raw.statistician: {"add_names": ["trimmed_mean_r0.30", "robust_median", "topk_mean_per_horizon_k5"], "remove_names": [], "params_overrides": {}, "rationale": "Current set has only 4 candidates with no baseline_mean (only selection/weighted). Universe shows trimmed_mean_r0.30 beats dba_combination on RMSE/SMAPE/MAPE and robust_median is strong on MAPE. relative_spread_mean=0.335≥0.25 favors weighted methods. n_windows=3≤4 suggests stronger shrinkage (trim_ratio). Adding trimmed_mean_r0.30 for overall performance, robust_median for stability (unstable flag), topk_mean_per_horizon_k5 for selection diversity.", "changes": ["added trimmed_mean_r0.30 for best overall RMSE/SMAPE/MAPE", "added robust_median for MAPE and stability", "added topk_mean_per_horizon_k5 for selection diversity"], "when_good": ""}

pattern_analyst.cot_narrative: The analysis reveals significant model disagreement (rmse_spread_ratio=0.47) and high seasonality variance (0.65), with ARIMA best tracking trends while ONLY_FT_catboost excels at seasonal patterns. Early horizons favor FT_svr/FT_catboost, while late horizons prefer catboost/ETS/THETA. These divergent capabilities across models support dba_combination as the recommended approach to leverage complementary strengths.

proposer.rationale: n_unique_winners=6 indicates significant model disagreement across horizons. relative_spread_mean=0.335 (≥0.25) favors robust methods like dba_combination and inverse_rmse_weights. Pattern analyst recommends 'dba_combination' for handling phase shifts with high seasonality variance (0.65). n_windows=3 suggests small top_k=3 with shrinkage=0.35 to avoid overfitting. DWT_rf (RMSE=1508) vs NaiveSeasonal (RMSE=2135) shows ~41% RMSE spread, supporting weighted approaches.

skeptic.rationale: Current set has 4 candidates with diverse types: baseline (dba_combination), selection (topk_mean_per_horizon_k3, best_per_horizon_by_validation), weighted (inverse_rmse_weights_k3_sh0.35). Diversity rule satisfied (≥2 different types). No leakage detected - all are in candidate_ranking_top from the packet. Universe leaderboards show trimmed_mean variants as top baselines but current set already has baseline coverage. Score margin between top2 is 0.018, indicating good differentiation. No pruning needed (4 candidates < threshold of ≥4 same type). All names valid.

statistician.rationale: Current set has only 4 candidates with no baseline_mean (only selection/weighted). Universe shows trimmed_mean_r0.30 beats dba_combination on RMSE/SMAPE/MAPE and robust_median is strong on MAPE. relative_spread_mean=0.335≥0.25 favors weighted methods. n_windows=3≤4 suggests stronger shrinkage (trim_ratio). Adding trimmed_mean_r0.30 for overall performance, robust_median for stability (unstable flag), topk_mean_per_horizon_k5 for selection diversity.
changes: added trimmed_mean_r0.30 for best overall RMSE/SMAPE/MAPE
added robust_median for MAPE and stability
